hook: $100K Income: How Much House Can You Afford?
title: How Much House Can You Afford on $100K Income? — Free Calculator
description: On a $100,000 annual income, you can typically afford a home between $300,000 and $400,000 depending on your down payment, debts, and interest rate. This free calculator factors in taxes, insurance, PMI, and HOA fees to give you your exact number — not a rough estimate.
target_keyword: how much house can i afford on 100k income

visual.headline:
$100K Income:
How Much House?
visual.center_card: GIANT NUMBER: '$300K–$400K' (80px bold, teal on white card)
visual.assumptions: Based on 28% DTI, 20% down, 6.5% rate, $600/mo debts
visual.colors: White background, teal (#0F766E) accent, white card with subtle shadow

image_prompt: Create a premium vertical Pinterest pin (1000x1500px) for a finance calculator website. Clean modern fintech design, white/light gray background, mobile-first layout. TOP: small QFINHUB text mark. HEADLINE in large 52px bold dark slate text: '$100K Income: How Much House Can You Afford?' CENTER: a clean white card with rounded corners (16px radius) and subtle shadow containing a giant number '$300K–$400K' in 80px bold teal (#0F766E) color, centered. Below the number on the card, small 16px gray text: 'Based on 28% DTI, 20% down, 6.5% rate, $600/mo debts'. BOTTOM: a rounded pill CTA button in teal fill with white 28px semibold text: 'Calculate Your Exact Number →'. Small QFINHUB text bottom-right in subtle gray. No people, no photos, no clutter, no stock images, no AI illustrations. Pure financial dashboard aesthetic.
why_better: Giant number card gives immediate answer — saves the user from mental math. 'Your Exact Number' CTA is specific and personal. No formula slugs, no clutter, just the answer with a path to precision.

hook: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche: Which Pays Off Faster?
title: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche Method — Which Pays Off Faster? Free Comparison
description: The avalanche method saves $2,347 more in interest on a $30K debt, but the snowball method pays off the first debt 8 months sooner. Which is right for you? Compare both strategies side by side with your exact debts — free, instant results.

visual.headline:
Snowball vs
Avalanche
visual.left_card: SNOWBALL — 'Pays first debt 8 months sooner' (36px bold) — Best if: you need quick wins for motivation
visual.right_card: AVALANCHE ★ — 'Saves $2,347 more' (36px bold) — Best if: you want to minimize total interest paid

image_prompt: Create a premium vertical Pinterest pin (1000x1500px) for a finance comparison. Clean modern fintech design, white/light gray background. TOP: small QFINHUB text. HEADLINE large 48px bold: 'Debt Snowball vs Avalanche: Which Pays Off Faster?' CENTER: Two cards side by side with 16px gap. LEFT CARD (white, subtle border): 'SNOWBALL' label above, 'Pays first debt 8 months sooner' in 36px bold dark text, below in 14px: 'Best if: you need quick wins for motivation'. RIGHT CARD (white, subtle green glow border, ★ badge top-right): 'AVALANCHE ★' label, 'Saves $2,347 more' in 36px bold green (#059669) text, below in 14px: 'Best if: minimize total interest'. Below cards: thin green strip across width reading 'Winner: Avalanche saves $2,347 more interest' in 18px white text. BOTTOM: CTA pill button 'Compare Both Free →'. QFINHUB at bottom. No people, no photos, no clutter.
why_better: Side-by-side comparison with clear winner (★ badge) gives immediate value. Specific dollar savings make it concrete. 'Compare Both Free' invites interaction without commitment.

hook: Before You Buy a Home, Check These 7 Numbers
title: 7 Numbers to Check Before Buying a Home — Free Calculator Checklist
description: Before you start house hunting, calculate these 7 numbers: 1) Credit score, 2) Debt-to-income ratio, 3) Down payment needed, 4) Monthly payment estimate, 5) Closing costs, 6) Emergency fund after purchase, 7) Total cost over 30 years. Use our free calculators for all 7 — instant results.
target_keyword: what to check before buying a home financially

visual.headline:
Before You Buy
a Home, Check
These 7 Numbers
checklist_items: ③ Down Payment — 3.5% FHA or 20% conventional
④ Monthly Payment — PITI + PMI + HOA
⑤ Closing Costs — budget 2-5% of price

image_prompt: Create a premium vertical Pinterest pin (1000x1500px) for a home-buying checklist. Warm cream/off-white background (#FFFBEB). TOP: small QFINHUB text mark. HEADLINE 48px bold dark slate, 3 lines: 'Before You Buy a Home, Check These 7 Numbers'. CENTER: 7 checklist items stacked vertically with 12px gaps. Each item: green (#059669) circle with white number (① through ⑦), label in 18px semibold dark text, small 13px gray explainer below. Items: ① Credit Score — 620+ for conventional, ② DTI Ratio — keep under 36%, ③ Down Payment — 3.5% FHA or 20% conventional, ④ Monthly Payment — PITI + PMI + HOA, ⑤ Closing Costs — budget 2-5% of price, ⑥ Emergency Fund — 3 months after purchase, ⑦ 30-Year Cost — principal + interest. BOTTOM: CTA pill button green fill white text 'Calculate All 7 Free →'. QFINHUB bottom-right. Clean, saveable, no clutter.
why_better: Checklists are Pinterest's most-saved format. Numbered circles with specific benchmarks (620+ credit, 36% DTI) make this genuinely useful — users save it for later reference, driving long-tail traffic.

hook: 5 Mortgage Mistakes That Make a Home Unaffordable
title: 5 Mortgage Mistakes That Make Your Home Unaffordable — Avoid These
description: Most homebuyers make at least one of these costly mistakes: 1) Maxing out your budget, 2) Skipping rate shopping, 3) Ignoring PMI costs, 4) Forgetting closing costs, 5) Not checking your credit first. Use our free mortgage calculator to see your actual affordable number.
target_keyword: mortgage mistakes to avoid when buying a home

visual.headline:
5 Mortgage Mistakes
That Make a Home
Unaffordable
mistake_items: ⚠ Maxing Out Your Budget — leaves no room for repairs or life changes
⚠ Skipping Rate Shopping — 0.5% difference = $30K+ over 30 years
⚠ Forgetting Closing Costs — 2-5% of purchase price, upfront
⚠ Not Checking Credit First — lower score = higher rate = thousands lost

image_prompt: Create a premium vertical Pinterest pin (1000x1500px) warning about mortgage mistakes. Light red-tinted white background (#FEF2F2). TOP: small QFINHUB text. HEADLINE 46px bold dark slate, 3 lines: '5 Mortgage Mistakes That Make a Home Unaffordable'. CENTER: 5 warning items stacked vertically. Each: red (#DC2626) left border accent line on a white card, ⚠ icon, mistake title in 17px bold dark text, cost/impact in 13px gray below. Items: ⚠ Maxing Out Your Budget — no room for repairs, ⚠ Skipping Rate Shopping — 0.5% diff = $30K+/30yr, ⚠ Ignoring PMI — adds $100-300/month, ⚠ Forgetting Closing Costs — 2-5% upfront, ⚠ Not Checking Credit — lower score = thousands lost. BOTTOM: CTA pill button red fill white text 'Check Your Affordable Number →'. QFINHUB bottom-right. Urgent but trustworthy, no fear-mongering, no stock photos.
why_better: Mistake pins trigger 'I better save this' response. Specific dollar impacts ($30K+, $100-300/mo) make the warnings concrete rather than abstract. Red accent draws eye in feed.

hook: $400K Home at 6.5%: What's the Monthly Payment?
title: $400,000 Home at 6.5% Interest — Your Monthly Payment Breakdown
description: A $400,000 home with 20% down at 6.5% APR equals approximately $2,022/month principal & interest. With taxes, insurance, and PMI, expect $2,500-2,800/month total. See your exact payment for any home price, rate, or down payment — free instant calculator.
image_prompt: Create a premium vertical Pinterest pin (1000x1500px) showing a realistic calculator result. Clean white/light gray background, modern fintech dashboard style. TOP: small QFINHUB text. HEADLINE 48px bold dark slate: '$400K Home at 6.5%: What's the Monthly Payment?' CENTER: a clean white calculator result card with subtle shadow and 16px rounded corners. Card layout: INPUTS section with 3 rows — 'Home Price: $400,000', 'Down Payment: 20% ($80K)', 'Interest Rate: 6.5%' — each with small 14px gray label above 18px dark value. Thin divider line. RESULT section: 'Monthly P&I' label in 16px gray above GIANT '$2,022' in 64px bold teal (#0F766E) with '/month' label. Below: small 14px gray 'With taxes + insurance: ~$2,600/mo'. Below card: 15px teal link-style text 'With 3.5% down (FHA): $2,502/mo →'. BOTTOM: CTA pill teal fill white text 'Calculate Your Payment →'. QFINHUB bottom-right. No screenshots, no browser chrome, no clutter. Pure dashboard calculator aesthetic.
why_better: The calculator-result format gives a 'preview' of the tool without looking like a screenshot. Specific numbers ($2,022, $2,600) answer the question immediately. Alternative scenario (FHA at 3.5%) teases additional value if they click through.
